description: composer.lock is missing or gitignored, so each environment resolves a different dependency graph and prod runs untested versions.
body: Failure mode: an application does not commit composer.lock (or deploy runs composer update), so CI, staging, and production each resolve constraints independently and run different transitive versions than were tested. Detection signals: composer.lock absent or in .gitignore for an app; deploy scripts call composer update; a bug reproduces only in one environment. Do not resolve versions at deploy time for an application. Safer fallback: commit composer.lock, run composer install (which honors the lock) everywhere, and reserve composer update for a reviewed, tested dependency bump. Libraries legitimately omit the lock; applications must not.
